rationale: The cited paper (DOI-matched: 10.1509/jmkr.48.SPL.S138) is not just topically adjacent: it studies how borrower narratives/identity claims affect peer-to-peer lending outcomes beyond objective financial information, while the citing paper examines psychological drivers of lender choice on Kiva beyond financial variables. Given this clear conceptual overlap in mechanisms and setting (online prosocial/P2P lending), the citation could plausibly serve as a theory or comparison source rather than an incidental mention.
